title: vepris eugeniifolia
intro: Vepris eugeniifolia, also known as the Zanzibar orange or yellow-berried ironwood, is a plant species in the Rutaceae family, native to the tropical regions of East Africa. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities, derived from its essential oils and flavonoids. The health benefits of Vepris eugeniifolia include reducing fever, treating various infections, and alleviating respiratory issues, making it a sought-after herbal remedy in traditional medicine. Vepris eugeniifolia can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or oil, which can be used topically or taken orally to harness its medicinal properties.
